meta.name: UCLA Loneliness Scale
meta.description: The UCLA Loneliness Scale is a 20-item measure assessing subjective feelings of loneliness and social isolation.
meta.author: Russell D (1996)
meta.reference: Russell D. UCLA Loneliness Scale (Version 3): Reliability, validity, and factor structure. J Pers Assess. 1996;66(1):20-40.
meta.instruction: Indicate how often each of the statements below is descriptive of you.

questions.text: I feel in tune with the people around me.
I lack companionship.
There is no one I can turn to.
I do not feel alone.
I feel part of a group of friends.
I have a lot in common with the people around me.
I am no longer close to anyone.
My interests and ideas are not shared by those around me.
I am an outgoing person.
There are people I feel close to.
I feel left out.
My social relationships are superficial.
No one really knows me well.
I feel isolated from others.
I can find companionship when I want it.
There are people who really understand me.
I am unhappy being so withdrawn.
People are around me but not with me.
There are people I can talk to.
There are people I can turn to.

ranges.label: Low Loneliness
ranges.description: Your loneliness level is low. You appear to have satisfying social connections.
suggestions: Consider helping others who may feel lonely

ranges.label: Moderate Loneliness
ranges.description: You have a moderate level of loneliness and may feel socially lacking in some areas.
suggestions: Actively participate in social activities and interest groups
Consider community involvement such as volunteering

ranges.label: High Loneliness
ranges.description: You have a high level of loneliness that may affect your mental health.
suggestions: Try changing social patterns to increase meaningful social interactions
Social skills training may be helpful

ranges.label: Severe Loneliness
ranges.description: You have severe loneliness that may significantly affect your physical and mental health.
suggestions: Professional psychological help is strongly recommended
Loneliness can be improved through treatment
If experiencing depressive symptoms, attend to your emotional health as well
